{{神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:宗神社 本殿.JPG|280px]]
本殿 | 名称 = 宗神社 | 所在地 = 京都府南丹市八木町屋賀南永寿34 | 緯度度 = 35|緯度分=04|緯度秒=6.87 | 経度度 = 135|経度分=33|経度秒=11.53 | 祭神 = [[天児屋根命]] | 社格 = 式内社?
[[総社]]? | 創建 = 不詳 | 本殿 = [[流造|一間社流造]] | 別名 = 宗社大明神 | 例祭 = | 神事 = }} '''宗神社'''（そうじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[南丹市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]推定地で、[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地。旧称'''宗社大明神'''または'''宗社'''。 __TOC__
== 祭神 == * [[天児屋根命]] == 歴史 == 神社のある南丹市八木町屋賀は[[丹波国|丹波]][[国府]]推定地の一つで、宗神社は総社であったと推定されている。また、桑田郡内で比定地のない[[式内社]]・三縣（みあかた）神社が、当社ではないかとも推定されている 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[平凡社]]）丹波国項。。 本殿は[[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）建立。 == 境内 == 境内は屋賀の集落の南部にあり、本殿が北面する北面神社である。 === 境内社 === * 三輪大神神社 - 祭神：[[大物主|大物主命]]。昭和33年創建。 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[八木駅]]または[[千代川駅]]から、車で約10分。 == 周辺 == * [[出雲大神宮]] - [[丹波国]][[一宮]]。 * [[丹波国分寺]] * [[千歳車塚古墳]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja 宗神社]（南丹生活～南丹市総合ガイド） * [http://www.genbu.net/data/tanba/sou_title.htm 宗神社（八木町）]（[http://www.genbu.net/ 玄松子の記憶]（個人サイト）） * [http://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/soujinja.html 宗神社]（[http://kuchitanshrine.my.coocan.jp/index.html 口丹波の社]（個人サイト）） {{デフォルトソート:そうしんしや}} [[Category:南丹市の神社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:式内小社]]

[[丹波国]][[総社]]? |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = 宗社大明神 |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''宗神社'''（そうじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]]にある[[神社]]。旧称は'''宗社大明神'''または'''宗社'''。[[式内社]]参考地で、[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地。 == 祭神 == * [[天児屋根命]] == 歴史 == 神社のある南丹市八木町屋賀は、[[丹波国|丹波]][[国府]]推定地の一つである''（[[丹波国#国内の施設]]も参照）''。この説の中で、宗神社は総社であったと考えられている。 また『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載された「丹波国桑田郡 三縣（みあかた）神社」の比定地がなく、当社ではないかと推測されている 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[平凡社]]）丹波国府跡項。。 == 境内 == 境内は屋賀の集落の南部に位置する。集落に向かって本殿が北面する北面神社である。 * 本殿 - [[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）建立 ; 境内社 * 三輪大神神社 - 祭神：[[大物主|大物主命]]。昭和33年創建 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[八木町 (京都府)|八木町]]屋賀南永寿34 ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[八木駅]]または[[千代川駅]]から、車で約10分 ; 周辺 * [[出雲大神宮]] - [[丹波国]][[一宮]] * [[丹波国分寺]] * [[千歳車塚古墳]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja 宗神社]（南丹生活～南丹市総合ガイド） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:そうしんしや}} [[Category:南丹市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:総社]]

[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = 宗社大明神 |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''宗神社'''（そうじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]]八木町屋賀にある[[神社]]。旧称は'''宗社大明神'''または'''宗社'''。[[式内社]]参考地で、[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地。 == 祭神 == * '''[[天児屋根命]]''' （あめのこやねのみこと） == 歴史 == 神社のある南丹市八木町屋賀は、[[丹波国|丹波]][[国府]]推定地の1つである（「[[丹波国#国内の施設]]」も参照）。この説の中で、宗神社は総社であったと考えられている。 また『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載された「丹波国桑田郡 三縣（みあかた）神社」の比定地がなく、当社ではないかと推測されている 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[平凡社]]）丹波国府跡項。。 == 境内 == 境内は屋賀の集落の南部に位置する。集落に向かって本殿が北面する北面神社である。 * 本殿 - [[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）建立 File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|鳥居 == 摂末社 == * 三輪大神神社 - 祭神：[[大物主|大物主命]]。昭和33年創建 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[京都府]][[南丹市]]八木町屋賀南永寿34 ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[八木駅]]または[[千代川駅]]から、車で約10分 ; 周辺 * [[出雲大神宮]] - [[丹波国]][[一宮]] * [[丹波国分寺]] * [[千歳車塚古墳]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja 宗神社]（南丹生活～南丹市総合ガイド） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:そうしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:南丹市]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:丹波国|社0]]

[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = 宗社大明神 |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''宗神社'''（そうじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]]八木町屋賀にある[[神社]]。旧称は'''宗社大明神'''または'''宗社'''。[[式内社]]参考地で、[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地。 == 祭神 == * '''[[天児屋根命]]''' （あめのこやねのみこと） == 歴史 == 神社のある南丹市八木町屋賀は、[[丹波国|丹波]][[国府]]推定地の1つである（「[[丹波国#国内の施設]]」も参照）。この説の中で、宗神社は総社であったと考えられている。 また『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載された「丹波国桑田郡 三縣（みあかた）神社」の比定地がなく、当社ではないかと推測されている 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[平凡社]]）丹波国府跡項。。 == 境内 == 境内は屋賀の集落の南部に位置する。集落に向かって本殿が北面する北面神社である。 * 本殿 - [[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）建立 File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|鳥居 == 摂末社 == * 三輪大神神社 - 祭神：[[大物主|大物主命]]。昭和33年創建 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[京都府]][[南丹市]]八木町屋賀南永寿34 ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[八木駅]]または[[千代川駅]]から、車で約10分 ; 周辺 * [[出雲大神宮]] - [[丹波国]][[一宮]] * [[丹波国分寺]] * [[千歳車塚古墳]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja 宗神社]（南丹生活～南丹市総合ガイド） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:そうしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:丹波国|社0]]

__NOTOC__ {{神社 |画像 = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|300px]]
本殿 |名称 = 宗神社 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[八木町 (南丹市)|八木町]][[屋賀]][[南永寿]]34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=宗神社}} |祭神 = [[天児屋根命]]（[[春日権現]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）参考地
[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = 宗社大明神 |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''宗神社'''（そうじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[八木町 (南丹市)|八木町]][[屋賀]][[南永寿]][[船井郡|舩井郡]]（[[丹波亀山藩|龜山藩]]）[[屋賀村]]、[[本荘村 (京都府)|本莊村]]屋賀、[[富本村 (京都府)|富本村]]の各一部にある[[神社]]。旧称は'''宗社大明神'''または'''宗社'''。[[式内社]]参考地で、[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地。 == 祭神 == * '''[[天児屋根命]]''' （あめのこやねのみこと） == 歴史 == 神社のある南丹市八木町屋賀は、[[丹波国|丹波]][[国府]]推定地の1つである（「[[丹波国#国内の施設]]」も参照）。この説の中で、宗神社は総社であったと考えられている。 また『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載された「丹波国桑田郡 [[三縣神社|三縣（みあかた）神社]]」の比定地がなく、当社ではないかと推測されている 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[平凡社]]）丹波国府跡項。。 == 境内 == 境内は屋賀の集落の南部に位置する。集落に向かって本殿が北面する北面神社である。 * 本殿 – [[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）建立 File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|鳥居 == 摂末社 == * 三輪大神神社 – 祭神：[[大物主|大物主命]]。昭和33年創建 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[八木町 (南丹市)|八木町]][[屋賀]][[南永寿]]34 ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[八木駅]]または[[千代川駅]]から、車で約10分 ; 周辺 * [[寳樹山安樂寺]] – 丹波[[国府]]の一つ * 旧[[山陰道]] ** [[出雲大神宮]] – [[丹波国]][[一宮]] ** [[丹波国分寺]]（護國山國分僧寺・國分尼寺） – [[国分寺]] ** [[千歳車塚古墳]] – [[千歳町 (亀岡市)|千歳町]][[千歳車塚]]。古墳時代後期としては丹波地方で最大規模 ** [[丹波七福神]] – 旧山陰道東麓 == 関連項目 == [[ファイル:Kugi nuki01.svg|200px|right|thumb|[[総代]]を務める川勝家の紋章]] * [[国府八幡宮]] * [[川勝氏]] * [[幡日佐氷室両神社|幡日佐・氷室両神社]] – 八木町[[氷所]] * [[徳雲寺 (南丹市)|鹽田山德雲禪寺]]（[[荘林氏]]（[[藤原氏]]）建立） * [[舩井神社]] – 八木町[[八木町船枝|船枝]]。船井郡[[一宮]][[丹波国の式内社一覧]] * [[京都帝釈天|醫王山福壽寺 京都帝釋天]] – 八木町[[八木町船枝|船枝]] * [[龍穏寺 (南丹市)|玉寶山龍穩寺]] – 園部町[[仁江]][[仁江甲|甲]]溝畑。この地の豪族（[[足立氏#丹波・足立氏|足立氏]][[金幡宗全]]）が建立、地元藩主[[家老]]の菩提寺。「船井の三うん寺」 * [[船井郡]] * [[桑田郡]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja 宗神社]（南丹生活～南丹市総合ガイド） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:そうしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:丹波国|社0]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:船井郡]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}}

本殿 |名称 = 宗神社 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[八木町 (南丹市)|八木町]][[屋賀]][[南永寿]]34 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=宗神社}} |祭神 = [[天児屋根命]]（[[春日権現]]） |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）参考地
[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[流造]] |別名 = 宗社大明神 |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''宗神社'''（そうじんじゃ）は、[[京都府]][[南丹市]][[八木町 (南丹市)|八木町]][[屋賀]][[南永寿]][[船井郡|舩井郡]]（[[丹波亀山藩|龜山藩]]）[[屋賀村]]、[[本荘村 (京都府)|本莊村]]屋賀、[[富本村 (京都府)|富本村]]の各一部にある[[神社]]。旧称は'''宗社大明神'''または'''宗社'''。[[式内社]]参考地で、[[丹波国]][[総社]]推定地。 == 祭神 == * '''[[天児屋根命]]''' （あめのこやねのみこと） == 歴史 == 神社のある南丹市八木町屋賀は、[[丹波国|丹波]][[国府]]推定地の1つである（「[[丹波国#国内の施設|丹波国内の施設]]」も参照）。この説の中で、宗神社は総社であったと考えられている。 また『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載された「丹波国桑田郡 [[三縣神社|三縣（みあかた）神社]]」の比定地がなく、当社ではないかと推測されている 『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[平凡社]]）丹波国府跡項。。 == 境内 == 境内は屋賀の集落の南部に位置する。集落に向かって本殿が北面する北面神社である。 * 本殿 – [[寛永]]15年（[[1638年]]）建立 File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|拝殿 File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|鳥居 == 摂末社 == * 三輪大神神社 – 祭神：[[大物主|大物主命]]。昭和33年創建 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[京都府]][[南丹市]][[八木町 (南丹市)|八木町]][[屋賀]][[南永寿]]34 ; 交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[嵯峨野線]] [[八木駅]]または[[千代川駅]]から、車で約10分 ; 周辺 * [[寳樹山安樂寺]] – 丹波[[国府]]の一つ * 旧[[山陰道]] ** [[出雲大神宮]] – [[丹波国]][[一宮]] ** [[丹波国分寺]]（護國山國分僧寺・國分尼寺） – [[国分寺]] ** [[千歳車塚古墳]] – [[千歳町 (亀岡市)|千歳町]][[千歳車塚]]。古墳時代後期としては丹波地方で最大規模 ** [[丹波七福神]] – 旧山陰道東麓 == 関連項目 == [[ファイル:Kugi nuki01.svg|200px|right|thumb|[[総代]]を務める川勝家の紋章]] * [[国府八幡宮]] * [[川勝氏]] * [[幡日佐氷室両神社|幡日佐・氷室両神社]] – 八木町[[氷所]] * [[徳雲寺 (南丹市)|鹽田山德雲禪寺]]（[[荘林氏]]（[[藤原氏]]）建立） * [[舩井神社]] – 八木町[[八木町船枝|船枝]]。船井郡[[一宮]][[丹波国の式内社一覧]] * [[京都帝釈天|醫王山福壽寺 京都帝釋天]] – 八木町[[八木町船枝|船枝]] * [[龍穏寺 (南丹市)|玉寶山龍穩寺]] – 園部町[[仁江]][[仁江甲|甲]]溝畑。この地の豪族（[[足立氏#丹波・足立氏|足立氏]][[金幡宗全]]）が建立、地元藩主[[家老]]の菩提寺。「船井の三うん寺」 * [[船井郡]] * [[桑田郡]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja 宗神社]（南丹生活～南丹市総合ガイド） {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:そうしんしや}} [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:丹波国|社0]] [[Category:南丹市の歴史]] [[Category:船井郡]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall |name = Sō Shrine |address = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * Main Hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. Established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; Address * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City) {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sou Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Historical sites in Nantan]] [[Category:Sōja]] [[Category:Tanba Province|Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Nantan]]

{{Shrine |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
Main Hall |name = Sō Shrine |address = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * Main Hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; address * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City) {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sou Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Historical sites in Nantan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Tanba Province|Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Nantan]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] revision.comment: trimming down the excessive presence of categories from erroneous implementations
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] revision.comment: trimming down the excessive presence of categories from erroneous implementations
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sou Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Historical sites in Nantan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tanba Province|Shrine]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Buildings in Nantan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sou Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Historical sites in Nantan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]]{{Sōja shrines}}[[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Tanba Province|Shrine]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Buildings in Nantan]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:Images]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Sō Shrine (Nantan)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sou Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG|280px]]
main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City) {{Shinto}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sou Shrine}} [[Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Sōja main hall |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|04|06.87|N|135|33|11.53|E|region:JP|name=Sō Shrine}} |deity = [[Ame-no-Koyane]] |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|en|Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified }} '''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate == Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33). == Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan ; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en}}. ; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]] It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''. == Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) == History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region. Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。 == Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines). * main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15). <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11452023]] Wikidata name: Sō-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.068575|N|135.553203|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}} |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified |location = {{ill|Nantan|qid=Q170421}} }}
'''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan, Kyoto]], Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)

'''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan, Kyoto]], Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)

It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)

'''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan, Kyoto]], Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452023]] Wikidata name: Sō-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.068575|N|135.553203|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified |location = {{ill|Nantan|WD=Q170421}} }}
'''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan, Kyoto]], Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452023]] Wikidata name: Sō-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.068575|N|135.553203|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified |location = {{ill|Nantan|WD=Q170421}} }}
'''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of [[:en:Nantan, Kyoto|Nantan, Kyoto]], Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)
