<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)]][[ja:宗神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11452023]] Wikidata name: Sō-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.068575|N|135.553203|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified |location = {{ill|Nantan|WD=Q170421}} }}
'''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|WD=Q170421|lt=Nantan, Kyoto}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.068575|N|135.553203|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified |location = {{ill|Nantan|WD=Q170421}} }}
'''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|WD=Q170421|lt=Nantan, Kyoto}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)
{{Shinto}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) honden.JPG |name = Sō Shrine |location = Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = {{Coord|35.068575|N|135.553203|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} |status = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), hypothesized {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} |establishment = Unknown |main_hall = Single-bay nagare-zukuri style |former_name = Sōsha Daimyōjin, Sōsha |festivals = None specified |location = {{ill|Nantan|WD=Q170421|1=Nantan, Kyoto}} }}
'''Sō Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|宗神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in the Yaga neighborhood of {{ill|Nantan, Kyoto|en|Nantan, Kyoto|WD=Q170421|lt=Nantan, Kyoto|1=Nantan, Kyoto}}, Japan. It is a proposed {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} and a {{ill|Shikinaisha|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} referenced site. Historically, it was referred to as '''Sōsha Daimyōjin''' or simply '''Sōsha'''.
== Enshrined Deities == * '''[[Ame-no-Koyane]]''' ({{lang|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft}})
== History == The shrine is situated in the Yaga area, which is one of the hypothesized locations of the {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}} {{ill|Kokufu (provincial capital)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokufu (provincial capital)|qq=}}. Based on this theory, Sō Shrine is considered to have been the provincial {{ill|Sōja|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōja|qq=}} for the Tanba region.
Additionally, there is speculation that the shrine might correspond to the "Tanba Province Kuwata District Mikata Shrine" mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} due to the lack of a confirmed location for that shrine.<ref>『日本歴史地名大系 京都府の地名』（{{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}）丹波国府跡項。</ref>
== Grounds == The shrine grounds are located on the southern edge of the Yaga settlement, with the main hall facing north, in the orientation typical of {{ill|Hokumen Shrine|en|北面神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokumen Shrine|qq=}} (north-facing shrines).
* main hall - Built in 1638 (Kan'ei 15).
<gallery widths="200px" heights="150px"> File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) haiden.JPG|Worship Hall File:Sou-jinja (Nantan) torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>
== Auxiliary Shrines == * Miwa Ōkami Shrine - Enshrines [[Ōmononushi]]. established in 1958 (Shōwa 33).
== Visitor Information == ; location * Nananaga-34, Yagicho Yaga, Nantan City, Kyoto Prefecture, Japan
; Access * About 10 minutes by car from either {{ill|JR West|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}}'s {{ill|Sagano Line|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagano Line|qq=}} {{ill|Yagi Station (Kyoto)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagi Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} or {{ill|Chiyokawa Station|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyokawa Station|qq=}}.
; Nearby * [[Izumo Daijingū]] - {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}} of {{ill|Tanba Province|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Province|qq=}}. * [[Tanba Kokubunji]] * [[Chitose Kurazuka Kofun]]
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commons|Category:Sōja shrines]][[Category:Sou-jinja (Nantan)}} * [http://tanbarakuichi.sakura.ne.jp/nantan/sightseeing/sightseeing05.html#soujinja Sō Shrine] (Nantan Living - Comprehensive Guide to Nantan City)
{{Shinto}}
